title: Legal Certainty Regarding the Time Limit for Investigations in Relation to Delays in the Handling of Cases as the Subject of Pre-Trial Proceedings
author: Brya Sabatini,
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study aims to analyse how the absence of time limits creates legal uncertainty and opens the door to delays in the handling of cases; and to analyse whether delays in the handling of cases arising from the absence of time limits on investigations can be classified as a matter for pre-trial review. This study employs a normative legal research method. This study demonstrates that, firstly, the absence of such time limits causes investigations to become excessively lengthy, undermining due process of law and infringing upon the fundamental rights of complainants, victims and suspects; consequently, it is necessary to establish time limits of a maximum of 30 days for minor offences, 90 days for general offences, and 180 days for specific offences. Secondly, the practice of expanding the scope of pre-trial proceedings through Constitutional Court rulings, coupled with the absence of time limits, infringes upon the rights of complainants, victims and suspects, as they may become subjects of pre-trial proceedings under an open system subject to judicial interpretation. However, it is also necessary to expand the authority of pre-trial proceedings themselves in the future by amending the Criminal Procedure Code and incorporating time limits for case handling at the investigation stage.

Secondly, the practice of expanding the scope of pre-trial proceedings through Constitutional Court rulings, coupled with the absence of time limits, infringes upon the rights of complainants, victims and suspects, as they may become subjects of pre-trial proceedings under an open system subject to judicial interpretation. However, it is also necessary to expand the authority of pre-trial proceedings themselves in the future by amending the Criminal Procedure Code and incorporating time limits for case handling at the investigation stage.
keywords: Legal Certainty; Time Limit; Investigation; Pre-Trial; Criminal Procedure Code.
